step_by_step_thinking:
Unconjugated bilirubin would be expected to be increased in Crigger Najjar syndrome due to a complete lack of UGT1A1 activity, which leads to buildup of unconjugated bilirubin. In contrast, Dubin Johnson syndrome does not affect UGT1A1 and instead affects another aspect of bilirubin metabolism involving accumulation of melanoid pigments inside hepatocytes. Therefore, unconjugated bilirubin remains unaffected.
\boxed{B} Based on the given information about each condition and the role of UGT1A1 in bilirubin conjugation, unconjugated bilirubin would be normal in Dubin Johnson syndrome since it primarily affects biliary secretion rather than UGT1A1 activity.
